name: browserytools
description: 100 Essential browser-based tools for productivity. No servers. Full privacy. Convert files, compress images, generate passwords, format code, and more - all in your browser.
readme_summary: BrowseryTools is a collection of over 80 browser-based utility tools built with Next.js and TypeScript. These tools operate entirely client-side, ensuring user privacy and eliminating the need for server uploads or accounts. They cover a wide range of functionalities including image manipulation, file conversion, text processing, data handling, and calculations.
primary_use_case: Providing a private, offline suite of essential browser-based tools for everyday productivity tasks like file conversion, image editing, text manipulation, and data processing.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, open toolbox overflowing with diverse digital icons representing various tools (image, file, text, data, math). A friendly, stylized browser window is positioned above the toolbox, acting as a portal from which these tools emerge. Subtle glowing lines connect the browser to the toolbox, symbolizing the client-side processing. The background is clean and minimalist, perhaps a soft gradient, with the repository name 'BrowseryTools' subtly integrated into the scene. The overall mood is helpful and accessible, rendered in a clean, 3D isometric illustration style with bright, inviting colors.

name: linux-wallpaperengine
readme_summary: This project brings Wallpaper Engine-style live wallpapers to Linux, allowing users to run animated wallpapers from Steam's Wallpaper Engine on their desktop. It's an educational OpenGL-based wallpaper engine for Linux.
key_features: Run Steam Wallpaper Engine backgrounds on Linux
primary_use_case: To enable users to use animated wallpapers from Steam's Wallpaper Engine on Linux desktops.
cover_image_prompt: A digital artist sitting at a futuristic workstation, holding a glowing paintbrush. The paintbrush is actively painting a vibrant, animated scene onto a large monitor that displays a Linux desktop. Swirling particles of light and code emanate from the monitor, representing the live wallpaper. In the background, subtle icons of Steam and Linux are visible. The scene is illuminated by the glow of the monitor and the paintbrush, creating a magical and technological atmosphere. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a focus on the transformation of digital elements into a dynamic visual experience, with the repository name 'linux-wallpaperengine' subtly integrated into the workstation's interface.

name: unity-mcp
description: Unity MCP acts as a bridge, allowing AI assistants (like Claude, Cursor) to interact directly with your Unity Editor via a local MCP (Model Context Protocol) Client. Give your LLM tools to manage assets, control scenes, edit scripts, and automate tasks within Unity.
readme_summary: Unity MCP is a C# integration tool that bridges AI assistants with the Unity Editor. It allows LLMs to directly manage Unity assets, control scenes, edit scripts, and automate development tasks through the Model Context Protocol.
key_features: AI assistant integration (Claude, Cursor, VS Code, etc.)
Direct Unity Editor interaction via MCP
Extensive toolset for profiling, physics, build management, package management, graphics, and camera control
primary_use_case: Enabling AI assistants to directly interact with and control the Unity Editor for game development and other Unity-based projects.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old alchemist in a bustling Unity Editor workshop, holding a glowing orb (representing an AI assistant). With a wave of their hand, they are magically conjuring and arranging game assets, manipulating scene elements, and writing code snippets that float in the air. Small, ethereal streams of data connect the orb to various tools and windows within the Unity interface, such as the Hierarchy and Inspector. The alchemist's focus is on transforming raw data into a polished game scene. The scene is rendered in a vibrant, 3D isometric illustration style with a warm, inviting color palette, emphasizing the seamless blend of magic and technology.

name: Constrict
readme_summary: Constrict is a desktop application designed to compress video files to a specific target file size. It automates the process of calculating necessary encoding parameters like bitrate, resolution, and framerate, offering a user-friendly interface for bulk video compression.
key_features: Compress videos to target file sizes
Automatic calculation of ABR, resolution, framerate, and audio quality
Choice of codecs (H.264, HEVC, AV1, VP9)
primary_use_case: Compressing video files to meet specific size limits for uploads or storage, without manual trial-and-error.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled tailor meticulously shrinking a large, vibrant tapestry (representing a video file) down to a small, neatly folded square of a specific size. The tailor uses digital measuring tools and glowing threads that represent encoding parameters like bitrate and resolution. Small, subtle UI elements showing file size targets and compression progress are visible on a workbench nearby. The background is a clean, modern workshop with soft, focused lighting. The overall scene should convey precision and efficiency in reducing file size while preserving the essence of the original content. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with clear, friendly visuals.
